posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I got a problem with scanning width of the signal model. I do
combine -M MultiDimFit 2017UL_dimuon_SR2.txt --rMin 0 --rMax 10 --algo grid --points 100 -P width --floatOtherPOIs=1
python plot1DScan.py higgsCombineTest.MultiDimFit.mH120.root --POI width
and got error message:
In file included from input_line_174:1:
In file included from /cvmfs/cms.cern.ch/slc7_amd64_gcc900/lcg/root/6.22.08-ljfedo/include/CPyCppyy/API.h:30:
In file included from /cvmfs/cms.cern.ch/slc7_amd64_gcc900/external/python/2.7.15-bcolbf2/include/python2.7/Python.h:61:
In file included from /cvmfs/cms.cern.ch/slc7_amd64_gcc900/external/python/2.7.15-bcolbf2/include/python2.7/pyport.h:332:
In file included from /usr/include/sys/time.h:25:
/usr/include/bits/types/struct_timeval.h:8:8: error: redefinition of ‘timeval’
struct timeval
What is wrong ?  I did the same yesterday for SR1 and all was fine.
Thanks, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

I got a problem with scanning width of the signal model. I do

combine -M MultiDimFit 2017UL_dimuon_SR2.txt --rMin 0 --rMax 10 --algo grid --points 100 -P width --floatOtherPOIs=1

python plot1DScan.py higgsCombineTest.MultiDimFit.mH120.root --POI width

and got error message:

In file included from input_line_174:1:
In file included from /cvmfs/cms.cern.ch/slc7_amd64_gcc900/lcg/root/6.22.08-ljfedo/include/CPyCppyy/API.h:30:
In file included from /cvmfs/cms.cern.ch/slc7_amd64_gcc900/external/python/2.7.15-bcolbf2/include/python2.7/Python.h:61:
In file included from /cvmfs/cms.cern.ch/slc7_amd64_gcc900/external/python/2.7.15-bcolbf2/include/python2.7/pyport.h:332:
In file included from /usr/include/sys/time.h:25:
/usr/include/bits/types/struct_timeval.h:8:8: error: redefinition of ‘timeval’
struct timeval

What is wrong ? I did the same yesterday for SR1 and all was fine.

Thanks, Sasha.

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Can you confirm that you did cmsenv in the right place? It seems a library problem.
-Andrea
posts.cooked_html:
Can you confirm that you did cmsenv in the right place? It seems a library problem.

-Andrea

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
ah, yes. I did not do
cmssw-el7
cmsenv
now it works !
Thanks Andrea.
